frameworks.name: decentralized ledger / blockchain analogy
frameworks.one_line: linked list, hash functions, and sharding as metaphors for episodic memory mechanisms

frameworks.name: episodic memory
frameworks.one_line: mammalian episodic memory reframed via distributed-ledger primitives

frameworks.name: metacognition and reality monitoring
frameworks.one_line: introspective judgments cited as inspiration for probabilistic consensus

frameworks.name: probabilistic consensus protocols
frameworks.one_line: blockchain consensus possibly informed by perceptual conscious experience

core_question: Can computer-science primitives from blockchain (linked list, hash functions, sharding) illuminate neural mechanisms of episodic memory, and can brain phenomena reciprocally inform consensus protocol design?
key_assumptions: memory traces can be usefully analogized to chained, hashed, sharded ledger entries
metacognition and reality monitoring share computational structure with consensus mechanisms
interpretations: blockchain primitives are more relevant to episodic-memory neuroscience than previously recognized
brain phenomena (metacognition, reality monitoring, conscious perception) may inspire probabilistic consensus protocol design
